content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS of length NNN that contains only lowercase English letters.

You are also given a string PPP of length 262626, that contains every lowercase English letter exactly once.

You can perform the following operation:


Choose an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N).
Let x=27‚àíordP(Si)x = 27 - \text{ord}_P(S_i)x=27‚àíordP‚Äã(Si‚Äã).

Here, ordP(Si)\text{ord}_P(S_i)ordP‚Äã(Si‚Äã) denotes the (unique) position of character SiS_iSi‚Äã in string PPP.


Then, set SiS_iSi‚Äã to PxP_xPx‚Äã.
Is it possible to convert SSS into a string that's sorted in non descending order using this operation several times?

If it is possible, also find the minimum number of operations required.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS, consisting of lowercase English letters.
The third line of each test case contains the string PPP, which contains each lowercase English letter exactly once.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the minimum number of operations needed to convert SSS into a sorted string, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it's impossible to do so.
constraints: SSS and PPP contain only lowercase English letters, i.e, the characters `a, b, c, ..., z`.
PPP has length 262626, and Pi‚âÝPjP_i \neq P_jPi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Pj‚Äã for i‚âÝji \neq jiÓÄÝ=j.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

ordP(S2)=ordP(k)=15\text{ord}_P(S_2) = \text{ord}_P(\text{k}) = 15ordP‚Äã(S2‚Äã)=ordP‚Äã(k)=15.
x=27‚àí15=12x = 27 - 15 = 12x=27‚àí15=12.
So, we set S2S_2S2‚Äã to P12P_{12}P12‚Äã, which equals e.
The string is now "aeje".


Choose i=4i = 4i=4. Following the process, this will set S4S_4S4‚Äã to k, turning the string into "aejk" which is sorted in ascending order.

Test case 222: It can be proved that there's no way to sort SSS.
